caption:
Discovering your “why” is the compass that guides you through life’s journey. It’s the reason behind every choice you make, every goal you pursue, and every dream you chase. Take a moment to reflect on what truly ignites your passion and purpose. Embrace your “why” with clarity and conviction, and watch as it empowers you to achieve greatness. #FindYourWhy #PurposeDriven #PassionInProgress 🌟✨

We all go through Pain in life .. it what we chose to do with it !! Turn it into a Positive or just Choose to Suffer 😎
transcript: Hey guys, today I wanna share with you my "why". Why did I start training? Uh, what was the reason I got into this fitness, building my body and um, maintaining discipline and all of these things. So, um, it started off when I was very young. I was just a really skinny kid. I was a skinny fat. And um, I had a lot of girlfriends. Um, but I got picked on a lot. I lived like in a very posh area, but right next to it was a was the slum, was the fisherman that used to live there. And these kids would come picked on me, they would beat me up, they would push me around. They'd be like, "You're gonna get beaten up." You get what I'm saying? So, yeah, smoke blown on my face, disrespected, probably bullied. It was horrible. And um, I had no choice. Single mom, not from a very rich family as well. So I had to fend for myself. It was me, it was all up to me. It was, it was my choice. What am I gonna do? Am I gonna sit and take this bullshit? Am I gonna do something about it? I went to the gym, I started training. Um, put my head down, one year, I worked out, I worked out so hard. Every single morning, I went to this local gym. They had like iron weight, it was a rooftop. And it was really hard where I lived. So I'll go early in the mornings and get the workouts done. Boy, I'm telling you, after a year, when I got a little more bigger, when I walked around, those same people, "How are you?" And I liking a lot. That's what happened, bro. I got all that respect. I had changed my identity. I changed my whole personality. I had become a different person. I had evolved. I continue to evolved over the years. Now I'm 34 years old, and I've been training for maybe more than 15 years, plus, 16 years. And I totally love it. I'm so glad I made that choice to start training. We all have to find our pain, our "why". There is a reason that you wanna do this for, either for mental health or just to feel good or for your aesthetics or health reasons. It could be anything. Guys, find your "why", that is very important. And once I got into this, I loved it so much that I wanted to help other people. I want to help other people, and I don't wanna see them face those same situations that I faced. I wanna make them stronger. I want them to feel more confident. I want them to feel the best they could ever be, so that they don't have to doubt themselves, and don't ever get picked on. Let's go, come on!

caption:
God doesn’t build weak men.
He breaks you first.

Pain strips ego.
Suffering forges discipline.
Tests reveal who you really are.

Only then does strength appear.

I don’t explain this to my son.
I demonstrate it.
We climb.
We struggle.
We earn it.

This is how real strength is passed on.
Not through words.
Through example.
transcript: When God wants to make a man powerful, he don't crown him first, he breaks him. First, he strips away your comfort, your pride, your plans, till all that's left is what's real. That ain't punishment, that's training. See, pain don't come to destroy you, it comes to stretch what's weak inside you. So you can carry the weight of your destiny. Every bad day, every betrayal, every heartbreak. That's God molding you, piece by piece. You don't grow in comfort, you grow in chaos. The pressure that's breaking you now, that's the same pressure that'll make you unstoppable. And when you're down on your knees thinking it's over, it ain't the end, it's the rebuild. Most people quit when it hurts. But the chosen ones, they walk through the fire till their purpose starts glowing. Every tear, every trial.

caption:
Box jumps are not about looking athletic.
They are about staying powerful.

Jumping trains explosive strength.
It keeps fast-twitch muscle fibres alive.
It improves coordination, balance, and bone density.
It sharpens the nervous system.
It teaches your body to produce force quickly.

As we age, power is the first quality we lose.
Not strength.
Power.

When power drops, falls increase.
Reaction time slows.
Confidence in movement disappears.
Independence starts to fade.

Most adults stop jumping in their 30s.
By their 50s, they wonder why they feel fragile.

You don’t need heavy weights to stay young.
You need intent.
You need speed.
You need to remind your body what it was built to do.

Jump within your ability.
Progress intelligently.
Protect your future body today.

Train power now.
So life doesn’t feel heavy later.

caption:
Water can heal you.
And water can hurt you.
That’s what nobody tells you.

Your body is over 60% water.
Your brain, blood, muscles, joints, and organs rely on it to function.
But more is not always better.

Drink too little and performance drops fast.
Focus declines.
Strength fades.
Recovery slows.
Fat loss becomes harder.

Drink too much and you create a new problem.
You dilute sodium and electrolytes.
You feel bloated, foggy, weak.
Your nervous system loses balance.

So how much water do you actually need?

Forget “8 glasses a day.”
That rule ignores body size, movement, and sweat.

The real framework is simple.

Start with 30–35 ml per kg of bodyweight per day.
Train hard? Add more.
Sweat a lot? Add electrolytes, not just water.

Your urine doesn’t need to be crystal clear all day.
That’s a sign you may be overdoing it.

The real markers are better energy.
Clear thinking.
Good digestion.
Stable training performance.

Water is the foundation.
But hydration is water plus minerals.

Master this and everything improves.
Get it wrong and you’ll feel “off” no matter how hard you train.

This is why I say:
Before supplements.
Before fancy diets.
Get hydration right.

caption:
Your body follows orders.
Your life follows standards.
Both are controlled by the mind.

I’ve seen strong bodies trapped in weak thinking.
And I’ve seen average bodies do extraordinary things because the mind was disciplined.

Most people think their problem is motivation.
It’s not.
It’s conditioning.

From a young age, the mind is programmed.
Comfort over growth.
Excuses over ownership.
Distraction over focus.

So when life gets hard, they quit.
When results take time, they doubt.
When discipline is required, they negotiate.

The mind is the most powerful tool you own.
But only if you train it.

A weak mind looks for shortcuts.
A strong mind builds systems.

A weak mind waits to feel ready.
A strong mind acts despite fear.

A weak mind reacts to circumstances.
A strong mind dictates standards.

Every transformation I’ve witnessed started the same way.
A decision.
Not a feeling.

The moment you stop arguing with yourself.
The moment you keep promises to yourself.
The moment your words and actions finally align.

Train the body, yes.
But forge the mind.

Because once the mind is locked in,
the body has no choice but to follow.

This isn’t motivation.
This is mastery.

A bad night of sleep, stressful meeting, long commute, kids get sick, work gets heavy. And then suddenly, motivation gone. Because motivation, guys, is an emotion. And emotions tend to fade. It's not your fault, it's being human. But here is where the problem is. Most people build a plan that only works on the perfect days. When they feel confident, when they feel inspired, when they feel like they have time. But life? Let's get it straight, it's not perfect. So your habits must work when life is messy. The bad days, the low energy days, the stressful days, the days when you don't feel like it. Because if your systems requires motivation, your system is already broken. Let me say that again. If your plan requires you to feel like it, it's not a plan, it's a wish. High performers don't rise to motivation, they fall back on systems. And they don't ask, do I feel like training today? They ask, what is on the plan today? They don't freestyle nutrition, they repeat simple meals. They remove the decision fatigue. And they don't negotiate with their future, they protect it. I'm telling you, your future deserves a stronger you. Not the January version of you, the consistent version of you, the disciplined version of you. The version of you that shows up when it's hard. Habits beat hype, every single time. If in your past years, you keep repeating what you're doing, you're gonna be stuck. It is time to change the system. Because a new year won't change your life, only new standards will.
